question.text: What is the maximum period for which SEBI can attach bank accounts under Section 11(4) of the SEBI Act?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Section 11(4), SEBI can attach bank accounts or other property of any intermediary or any person associated with the securities market involved in violation of the SEBI Act provisions, for up to 90 days. **The Trap:** The 90-day limit is specific to bank account attachment under Section 11(4). Other remedies under the same section may have different timelines. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 90-day attachment is a powerful interim measure — it preserves assets during investigation. Compliance officers should be aware that violations can trigger immediate asset freezing.
